verses.commentary: Do not judge (me krinete, μὴ κρίνετε) — not 'never assess' but never condemn from a posture of superiority. The standard you apply returns on you.
Reciprocal measure: your verdicts boomerang. The judge invites the same scrutiny back. Justice is structured for symmetry.
Speck (karphos, κάρφος, splinter) versus log (dokos, δοκός, beam). The image is absurd — a beam protruding from your eye while you tweezer your neighbor's dust. Hypocrisy is comic before tragic.
Self-blind helper. You cannot extract what you cannot see, and the beam guarantees you cannot see.
Hypocrite — remove your own beam first. THEN you will see clearly to help. Critique is permitted, but only after self-criticism. Order matters.

Holy things to dogs, pearls to pigs — Old Testament unclean animals stand for those committedly hostile to the gospel. Discernment is not the opposite of grace; gospel proclamation requires wisdom about receptivity.
Ask, seek, knock — three present imperatives, escalating activity. Persistent prayer modeled. The promise is unequivocal: receive, find, opened.
'Everyone' (pas, πᾶς). No exception in the access. The Father's responsiveness is universal among askers.
Father-son analogy. Bread asked, stone given — no human father is that cruel. Petitions are taken seriously by parents.
Fish asked, snake given. Each example doubles the absurdity. Earthly parents do not betray basic needs.
A fortiori: 'you who are evil' (poneroi, πονηροί — fallen but parental) give good gifts. How much more (poso mallon, πόσῳ μᾶλλον) the heavenly Father? Confidence in prayer rests on God's fatherhood.
Golden Rule: do unto others. Frames the entire Law and Prophets. Positive formulation (do) goes further than negative versions found in Hillel and Confucius (don't do). Initiative replaces avoidance.
Two gates. Wide gate, broad road, many travelers — destruction (apoleian, ἀπώλειαν). Crowd-size is no measure of truth.
Narrow gate, hard road, few who find it — life. The minority of disciples is structurally normal, not a sign of failure.
False prophets — sheep on the outside, wolves within (lykoi harpages, λύκοι ἅρπαγες). Camouflage is their method. The threat is internal to the flock.
By their fruit (karpos, καρπός) recognize them. Behavior, not branding, is the test. Thorns cannot fake grapes.
Good tree, good fruit. Bad tree, bad fruit. Nature determines product. There is no neutral middle category.
Cannot — categorical. Trees cannot betray their kind. Heart-orientation is finally exposed by sustained output.
Trees without fruit are cut down and burned. Echoes John's preaching (3:10). Lukewarm middle ground does not exist in this taxonomy.
By fruit — the test repeated as conclusion. Discernment is empirical, patient, evidence-based.
Saying 'Lord, Lord' does not gain entry. Doing the Father's will does. Confession unaccompanied by obedience is empty noise.
'On that day' — final judgment. Prophecy, exorcism, miracles — done 'in your name.' Spiritual phenomena do not guarantee genuine relationship. Activity in Jesus' name can run on disconnected fuel.

'I never knew you' (oudepote egnon hymas, οὐδέποτε ἔγνων ὑμᾶς). Not 'I forgot' but 'I never knew.' Relationship, not performance, is the criterion. 'Workers of lawlessness' (anomian, ἀνομίαν) — gifted but disconnected from obedience.
Wise builder hears AND does. Rock foundation. The Sermon's payload is not information but practice.
Storm comes — rain, floods, winds. The house stands. Crisis reveals the foundation, does not create or destroy it.
Foolish builder hears but does not do. Sand foundation. The hearing alone does not save — same input, opposite outcome.
Same storm. House falls. 'Great was its fall' (megale, μεγάλη) — the collapse is total. Self-deception fails decisively under pressure.
First of five Matthean closing formulas ('when Jesus had finished'). Crowds amazed (exeplessonto, ἐξεπλήσσοντο — struck out of themselves). The sermon hit.

Authority (exousia, ἐξουσία) — not citation of authorities (as the scribes did) but personal warrant. Jesus speaks as the source, not the transmitter, of Torah. The chapter's climax names what the crowd sensed: this is a different kind of teacher.
